chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Criminal Bail Application No.761 of 2024 (Saqib v. The State) Date Order with signature of Judge For hearing of bail application 03.05.2024 Mr. Muhammad Yousuf, advocate for the applicant Mr. Muhammad Anwar Mahar, DDPP for the State ----------------------------------- It is alleged that the applicant with the rest of the culprits robbed complainant Waseem Akram of his mobile phone and cash worth rupees nine-lacs, and for that, the present case was registered. The applicant having been refused bail by learned IIIrd-Additional Sessions Judge, Karachi, Central, has sought the same from this Court by way of instant bail application under Section 497 Cr.P.C. Heard arguments and perused the record. The name of the applicant is not appearing in the FIR. The very case at one moment was disposed of by the police under `A` Class. The applicant has been subjected to an identification parade with a delay of five days. No explanation for such delay is offered. There is no recovery of robbed articles from the applicant. Complainant Waseem Akram by filing his affidavit before the learned trial Court has already recorded no objection to the grant of the bail to the applicant. The case has finally been challaned. There is no likelihood of absconsion or tampering with the evidence on the part of the applicant who is said to be in custody since seven months without effective progress in the trial of his case. In these circumstances, a case for the release of the applicant on bail on the point of further inquiry is made-out. Under the given circumstances, the applicant is admitted to bail subject to his furnishing surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Rupees Fifty Thousand only) and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the learned trial Court.

In these circumstances, a case for the release of the applicant on bail on the point of further inquiry is made-out. Under the given circumstances, the applicant is admitted to bail subject to his furnishing surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Rupees Fifty Thousand only) and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the learned trial Court. Instant bail application is disposed of accordingly. J U D G E
